explanation: This command simulates MSTSC shadowing activity with the required parameters 'shadow:' and 'noconsentprompt' as per the Sigma rule. It uses standard syntax and no obfuscation.
This command uses quotes to wrap the 'noconsentprompt' parameter, which is a safe mutation technique (insertion). The behavior remains unchanged, and it still matches the Sigma rule.
This command substitutes the long option format 'noconsentprompt' with a short format '-noconsentprompt' (substitution). The command is still valid and matches the Sigma rule.
This command reorders the arguments (reordering). The sequence of the command-line parameters is changed, but the behavior remains the same and matches the Sigma rule.
This command omits optional whitespace or formatting (omission) and uses standard syntax. It is a direct match to the Sigma rule.
This command uses command substitution to recode the 'noconsentprompt' parameter (recoding/encoding). The command remains valid and matches the Sigma rule.
